else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.

lees: noun. The sediment that settles during fermentation of beverages, consisting of dead yeast and precipitated parts of the fruit.

levy: verb. To impose (a tax or fine) to collect monies due, or to confiscate property.

lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).

lyse: verb. To burst or cut a cell or cell structure; to induce lysis.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reel: noun. A shaky or unsteady gait.
reeve: noun. Any of several local officials, with varying responsibilities.
rely: verb. To trust; to have confidence in; to depend.
resee: verb. To see again or anew.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
revel: noun. An instance of merrymaking; a celebration.
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seely: adjective. Alternative form of sely or silly.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.

sleeve: noun. The part of a garment that covers the arm.

slyer: adjective. comparative form of sly: more sly
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.

verse: noun. A poetic form with regular meter and a fixed rhyme scheme.
